# @meli/server-context

## Table of Contents

- [@meli/server-context](#meliserver-context)
  - [Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)
  - [Description](#description)
  - [Install](#install)
  - [Usage](#usage)
    - [Context Initialization](#context-initialization)
    - [Context Use](#context-use)
      - [Context Use - Functions](#context-use---functions)
      - [Context Use - Example](#context-use---example)

## Description

Module to create a Server Context which can be retrieved and used from any point within the app after its initialization.

This context is associated to the current incoming web request. For more information please refer to [Node's Asynchronous context tracking docs](https://nodejs.org/docs/latest/api/async_context.html#asynchronous-context-tracking).

## Install

```sh
npm install --save @meli/server-context
```

## Usage

```js
import { getReq, getRes } from '@meli/server-context';
```

```js
import { createContextMiddleware } from '@meli/server-context/middleware';
```

### Context Initialization

To initialize the Server Context, the `createContextMiddleware` function is provided, which returns the middleware to attach to your web server. This middleware initializes the store and links the context to the current request and response.

```js
import { createContextMiddleware } from '@meli/server-context/middleware';

router.use(createContextMiddleware());
```

> [!WARNING]  
> Beware that the server context is associated with the incoming web request, so trying to get the server context within a runtime that was NOT executed due to an incoming request, then the retrieved context will be `undefined`.

### Context Use

To get the context (`req` and `res`), use the `getReq` and `getRes` functions.

#### Context Use - Functions

| Function | Return Value                    | Description                                                                                                                       |
| -------- | ------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `getReq` | Express Request or `undefined`  | Returns the current request associated with the current context. Returns `undefined` if executed outside the associated context.  |
| `getRes` | Express Response or `undefined` | Returns the current response associated with the current context. Returns `undefined` if executed outside the associated context. |

#### Context Use - Example

```js
import { getReq, getRes } from '@meli/server-context';

const myService = () => {
  const req = getReq();
  const res = getRes();

  console.log(req.hostname);
  // www.mercadolibre.com.ar
  console.log(res.statusCode);
  // 200
};
```
